title: Hide multiple questions/columns at once in a Form View
excerpt: I have a board with multiple forms on it. It is very time consuming when setting up boards like this, because when I create a new form, it auto displays all the columns already in the board, and I have to go in and “hide…

posts.author: jennd (Jenn)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a board with multiple forms on it. It is very time consuming when setting up boards like this, because when I create a new form, it auto displays all the columns already in the board, and I have to go in and “hide question” on each and every question in the form. It would be very convenient for me to either:

a) select multiple questions at one time and easily toggle them to “hide”
b) have the option when creating a new form to import the current columns on the board as questions, or start blank and create new questions/columns

posts.author: trevor13 (Trevor Chauvin-DeCaro)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi! From what I can tell, Monday Forms don’t have a bulk hide option or the ability to start with a blank form versus pre-populated board columns.

Here are some tips and potenital workarounds you can try:

1. Use “Duplicate Form” as a Template

If you often need similar forms with most questions hidden, try this approach:

  • Create one “master form” with all the unnecessary questions hidden and your preferred setup complete.
  • When you need a new form, duplicate the form instead of creating from scratch.
  • Then, just tweak the visible questions—this can save a lot of time.

2. Use a Dedicated “Form Board”

Another workflow is to create a separate board just for form submissions:

  • Add only the columns that should appear as questions.
  • Build your form(s) there, so you’re not hiding dozens of extra columns.
  • Then use automations or connected boards/mirror columns to send or sync the data into your main board.

3. Feature Request: Bulk Hide & Blank Forms

Your idea is fantastic and aligns with many user requests I’ve heard:

  • Option A: Multi-select columns in the form builder to “hide” all at once.
  • Option B: Choose between “Start with all board columns” or “Start blank” when creating a new form.

I’d recommend submitting this to the Monday.com Feature Requests (or upvoting if someone’s already suggested it) because it’s the type of UX improvement they’re actively considering.

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @jennd

Our monday form builder app Easyform supports hiding all questions and then re-introducing specific questions, you can read about it more on our Adding Removing and Reordering Fields article.

You can check out or documentation here, if you want help setting up or have any questions, you can reach us at easyformsupport@easyapphq.com or book a call with us here.
